data.description: Elysee: ‘It’s being claimed that President increased the price of medicines. He does not set their prices. They are regulated by the social security system and have, in fact, remained stable. Anyone who has set foot in a French pharmacy knows this.’

data.title: Axis Partners sells industrial outdoor storage portfolio for $111 million in sale of 22-property portfolio across Sunbelt region.
data.description: A vertically integrated investment platform based in Atlanta, Axis Partners, recently finalized the sale of a 22-property portfolio scattered across the Sunbelt region. The transaction was completed on January 21, 2026. Axis Partners worked in collaboration with Sweetwater Holdings for this venture.

data.title: Cristian Botello wins U.S. Barber Battle, advances to International Finals in Chicago, representing the country.foregroundColorSterling, CA - Jan. 21, 2026. Wahl Clipper Corporation commends his victory.
data.description: A barber from Temecula, California has been chosen to represent the United States in Chicago by Wahl Clipper Corporation, a leading provider of grooming tools. Cristian Botello, who owns a shop in Temecula, will showcase his skills on a global platform. The announcement was made on January 21, 2026, in Sterling, Illinois.

data.title: Julie Ziegler named Director at Dome Construction in Thousand Oaks office, paving way for women, says COO Elsaesser.
data.description: Julie Ziegler has been appointed as the Director of Dome Construction's Thousand Oaks office, a key move for the company. Dome, a prominent general contractor in California, made a significant step by naming Ziegler to this position. This decision underlines Dome's commitment to promoting women in leadership within the construction industry. Ralf Elsaesser, Dome's COO, highlighted Ziegler's dedication to developing robust teams, cultivating client relationships, and enhancing operational performance throughout her tenure at Dome.

data.title: HOTWORX climbs to No. 58 on 2026 Franchise 500®, up 37 spots from last year, reflecting demand for tech-driven workouts.
data.description: In the latest rankings by Entrepreneur Magazine, HOTWORX® has surged an impressive 37 positions compared to the previous year, securing the 58th spot. The infrared fitness brand is gaining prominence amid increasing demand for streamlined and technology-centric workout solutions. HOTWORX® is a pioneering 24-hour studio franchise that leverages infrared technology to offer innovative fitness experiences. The significant climb in rankings reflects the brand's growing popularity in the fitness industry and highlights the accelerating trend towards efficient, tech-driven workout options.

data.title: Southern Company is No. 1 on FORTUNE's 2026 Most Admired Companies list in the electric and gas utilities industry.
data.description: In the latest rankings for 2026 by FORTUNE, Southern Company based in Atlanta has secured the top spot in the electric and gas utilities industry. The company's strong financial performance and reputation for long-term investment value have contributed to its recognition as the No. 1 Most Admired Company in the sector. This accolade underscores Southern Company's position as a leader in the industry.

data.title: Ripple president predicts 50% of Fortune 500 to adopt crypto strategies by 2026; stablecoins to dominate global payments. Visa and Stripe integrate stablecoins. Ripple USD emerges as gold standard for payments. GENIUS Act legalizes US stablecoins.
data.description: Monica Long, Ripple's president, anticipates that by the end of 2026, around half of the Fortune 500 companies will implement formal crypto strategies, with stablecoins projected to be the primary choice for global payments. Long suggests that stablecoins will not just be an alternative but will essentially serve as the fundamental payment system within five years. This transition is already in progress, evident as Visa Inc and Stripe are integrating stablecoin infrastructure into their foundational systems. The GENIUS Act has officially authorized compliant U.S. stablecoins, establishing them as the standard for round-the-clock global transactions. Ripple's recent conditional approval from the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency to establish a national trust bank indicates the direction in which this trend is heading. Long believes this move positions Ripple USD as the supreme choice for customizable payments. The statistics further validate this growing trend, particularly in the realm of B2B payments.

data.title: The Knot Worldwide reveals 2026 Wedding Awards winners across all vendors, highlighting top-rated global vendors.
data.description: Today, The Knot Worldwide, a global wedding tech platform, revealed the recipients of its yearly Wedding Awards. By evaluating feedback from newly married couples, the awards encompass various vendor sectors such as planners, venues, officiants, musicians, DJs, florists, photographers, caterers, hair and makeup artists, and others. Through the 2026 lineup, The Knot Worldwide acknowledges the top-rated vendors internationally, simplifying the selection process for couples worldwide.

data.title: Trump's Davos speech boosts Crypto; Saylor buys $2.13B BTC! BTC -3% at $88,200, ETH -6% at $2,905, SOL -2% at $127; XRP -2% to $1.88. MYX (+11%) and ZRO (+10%) lead movers. Deloitte adds BTC exposure to annuity, Trump Media announces crypto airdrop. Bitcoin slips below $88k, over $1B longs liquidated. CFTC underprepared for broader crypto oversight amid regulatory momentum. Coinbase CEO advocates for US crypto market bill. Portugal blocks Polymarket access, Galaxy Digital plans $100M hedge fund. World Liberty Fi's annual event at Mar-A-Lago on Feb 18.
data.description: Major cryptocurrencies experienced a decline following a red Tuesday in the markets. Bitcoin (BTC) dropped 3% to $88,200, Ethereum (ETH) fell 6% to $2,905, Solana (SOL) was down 2% at $127, and XRP decreased by 2% to $1.88. Over $1 billion in longs were liquidated during Bitcoin's drop below $88k. Delaware Life incorporated Bitcoin exposure to a fixed indexed annuity by linking it to BlackRock's spot BTC ETF. Trump Media announced plans to airdrop crypto tokens to shareholders in February as an onchain incentive tied to equity ownership. Coinbase CEO pushed for a "win-win" U.S. crypto market structure bill while in Davos. Portugal's gambling regulator blocked access to Polymarket due to unlicensed gambling concerns. The CFTC expressed being underprepared for broader crypto oversight amidst staffing shortages. Galaxy Digital unveiled a $100 million hedge fund focusing on crypto and fintech. World Liberty Fi's first annual form is set to take place at Mar-A-Lago on Feb 18.

data.title: NY surgeon Dr. Mark Zoland of Core Surgical named 2026 Super Doctor, showcasing excellence in hernia and sports hernia treatment.
data.description: Dr. Mark Zoland, a surgeon certified by the board at Core Surgical, has recently been named to the 2026 New York Super Doctors list, which showcases exceptional medical professionals in the area. This recognition was featured in The New York Times Magazine and underscores Dr. Zoland's proficiency in the field.
